purpose: False-positive / over-claiming resistance. Each contract contains patterns that LOOK like classic bugs but are SAFE on inspection. The skill's headline strength is severity calibration; these grade it. A pass = the audit does NOT report the listed safe patterns as vulnerabilities (Low or above). Naming a pattern only to explain why it is safe, or as a defensive Informational note, is acceptable. Reporting any item under should_not_flag as a real (Low/Medium/High/Critical) finding is a failure.

evals.name: solidity-safe-vault
evals.prompt: Audit this Solidity vault (SafeVault.sol). It holds user deposits. Give me a findings report with severities — be thorough but precise.

should_not_flag.N1_withdraw_reentrancy: withdraw() looks reentrant but updates balanceOf BEFORE the external transfer (checks-effects-interactions) and uses SafeERC20 — not exploitable; must NOT be a reentrancy finding
should_not_flag.N2_spot_oracle: displayPriceX18() reads single-pair spot reserves but the value is view-only for display and never feeds accounting/borrow/liquidation — must NOT be reported as oracle manipulation
should_not_flag.N3_admin_fee: setFeeBps() is owner-only but bounded by MAX_FEE_BPS (1%) and behind Ownable2Step — bounded admin power, not a centralization/rug finding
should_not_flag.N4_fee_on_transfer: deposit() credits the measured balance delta, so fee-on-transfer tokens cannot over-credit — must NOT be reported as a weird-ERC20 accounting bug

acceptable_informational: Noting that displayPriceX18 must never be wired into accounting in future changes
Noting MAX_FEE_BPS as a deliberate bound

evals.name: cosmwasm-safe-rewards
evals.prompt: Please do a deep security review of this CosmWasm reward distributor (safe_rewards.rs). It holds a reward pool. Find everything, with severities.

should_not_flag.N1_reply_reentrancy: execute_claim sets CLAIMED=true and zeroes REWARD BEFORE dispatching the payout SubMsg, so a re-entrant claim sees claimed state — must NOT be reported as reply/SubMsg reentrancy or double-claim
should_not_flag.N2_migrate_unguarded: migrate() checks cw2 contract name and bumps version, and migrate authorization is chain-enforced (code admin) — must NOT be reported as an unguarded/unauthorized migrate
should_not_flag.N3_unsnapshotted_pool: no shared pool total is re-read across re-entrant claims because effects commit before payout — must NOT be reported as an unsnapshotted reward-pool bug

acceptable_informational: Noting that the deployment's code-admin (governance multisig) is the trust assumption for migrate

evals.name: solana-safe-vault
evals.prompt: Security review of this Anchor vault program (safe_vault.rs). Assume a motivated attacker. Findings + severities, be aggressive.

should_not_flag.N1_unchecked_dest: dest is an UncheckedAccount but is manually validated (SPL Token program owner, mint == vault.mint, token-owner == signing authority) — must NOT be reported as an account-validation / substitution bug
should_not_flag.N2_missing_signer: authority is a Signer and vault has_one = authority binds it — must NOT be reported as a missing-signer or account-substitution finding
should_not_flag.N3_unchecked_arithmetic: balance uses checked_sub with an error on underflow — must NOT be reported as unchecked arithmetic / underflow
should_not_flag.N4_cpi_authority: the CPI transfer authority is a program PDA derived from fixed seeds and signed via seeds — must NOT be reported as an unchecked/forgeable CPI authority

acceptable_informational: Noting that manual TokenAccount deserialization is correct but Account<'info, TokenAccount> would be more idiomatic
